public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as quiet-room mediator.
appearance: Taren Mere usually presents in layered civic fabric cut to show rank without inviting touch, with details that make their quiet-room mediator status legible before introductions begin.
manner: Reads as a functional local operator shaped by place, duty, and the practical customs around them. In Transpolity, that reads as someone boundary-crossing and adaptive, speaking to whichever audience or protocol is presently in front of them, and the habits of a quiet-room mediator still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of quiet-room mediator in and around Silkweaver’s Chapel, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Silkweaver’s Chapel workable through the obligations of quiet-room mediator while managing the immediate pressure of institutional scrutiny in Transpolity.
hidden_pressure: Must keep their piece of the world working while larger systems push stress downward. Around Silkweaver’s Chapel, that usually means carrying institutional scrutiny, reputation risk, daily strain without letting the role of quiet-room mediator visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Getting involved with Taren Mere can expose the party to the everyday consequences of pressure that others only discuss in the abstract.
Taren Mere can offer grounded local knowledge once the party proves it is useful rather than merely curious.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained. V33_1 additive depth pass preserved canon while strengthening at-table identity, appearance, and social motive.
public_signal: Taren Mere carries the look of a practiced local operator whose authority is read through place, familiarity, and the reactions of others more than formal insignia.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Silkweaver’s Chapel: the part of Silkweaver’s Chapel where their work actually matters day to day
who_avoids_them: those who only appear when things break

In Transpolity, that reads as someone boundary-crossing and adaptive, speaking to whichever audience or protocol is presently in front of them, and the habits of a quiet-room mediator still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Quiet-Room Mediator, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Transpolity.
what_they_refuse: Taren Mere refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Quiet-Room Mediator too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Taren Mere can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Taren Mere adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Taren Mere is Known locally through day-to-day work as quiet-room mediator. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Quiet-Room Mediator.
